title: Boat Rental in Dominican Republic: Prices and Tips for 2026
html:
Hiring a yacht in the Dominican Republic offers access to the Caribbean Sea from Puerto Plata, with prices for a bareboat motorboat from €250 a day. BoatHire24 connects you with skippers and independent owners for a seamless booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Dominican Republic?

Prices for boat hire Dominican Republic start at about €100 per person for a shared tour and go up to €2,500 a day for a crewed luxury yacht. Half-day private motorboat rentals (4-6 hours) typically range from €250 to €500, while full-day catamaran charters for up to 12 guests cost between €800 and €1,500. For a premium experience, a crewed Sunseeker or Azimut yacht with captain and crew can exceed €2,000 per day. Always confirm fuel costs and insurance separately with the operator. You can boat hire Dominican Republic for your chosen dates.

Where to set off in Dominican Republic

The most popular departure points include Puerto Plata's Ocean World Marina, Cap Cana's Marina in Punta Cana, and the smaller Marina de la Casa de Campo in La Romana. Samaná Bay offers sheltered anchorage near Cayo Levantado, while Luperón Harbour provides access to the north coast. For day trips, head to Isla Saona from Bayahíbe or explore the pristine beaches of Cayo Arena from Puerto Plata. One option worth a look is the Olympic 490 listing.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat, Best for couples or small groups exploring coves; holds up to 6 guests; from €250 half-day.
  2. Catamaran, Ideal for party groups or family days out on the water; up to 12 guests; from €800 full-day.
  3. Crewed sailing yacht, Perfect for island-hopping to Isla Saona or Cayo Levantado; 8-10 guests; from €1,200 daily.
  4. Luxury motor yacht, Offers a premium experience on a vessel like an Azimut or Sunseeker; up to 12 guests; from €2,000 per day.

Costs at a glance

  • Price band: €100 (shared tour) to €2,500+ (crewded luxury yacht)
  • Peak months: December to April, when the trade wind provides steady breezes
  • Typical trip length: 4-8 hours half-day, 6-8 hours full-day
  • Maximum guests: Most boats carry 8-12 passengers; check local regulations
  • Currency: All prices quoted in EUR; payment generally accepted in USD or credit card

When to visit and do I need a licence?

The high season runs from December to April, when the trade wind provides steady breezes; low season offers lower prices but more rain. If you plan to captain a boat yourself, a recognised licence is required, but the operator will specify the exact requirements for each vessel. Most holidaymakers hire a private boat with a local skipper, which eliminates licence concerns.

To compare verified listings and book securely, use the boat hire Dominican Republic link on BoatHire24. For powerboat enthusiasts, the Olympic 490 listing on BoatHire24 shows a popular 16-foot speedboat available in the Puerto Plata area.

Questions about boat rental in Dominican Republic

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Dominican Republic?

Yes, you can hire a boat with a skipper who holds a valid local licence, which is the most common choice for tourists.

What is the best boat for a family day trip?

A catamaran or sailing yacht suits families best, as they offer stable platforms, shaded seating, and usually include a crewed service.

How far is Isla Saona from the mainland by boat?

Isla Saona lies roughly 15 nautical miles from the town of Bayahíbe, a journey of about 45 minutes by speedboat or one hour by catamaran.

What should I pack for a day charter in Dominican Republic?

Pack sunscreen, a hat, swimwear, a light jacket for wind, and cash for tips and any onboard purchases.

Whether you are a first-time renter or an experienced boater, BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Dominican Republic or list your own. Browse the current selection to plan your 2026 trip today.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Dominican Republic and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
